title: The Data Cleaning Pipeline
concept: Raw web text contains spam, gibberish, and toxic language; pretraining data must pass through classifiers to filter out low-quality pages.

exercises.prompt: What must developers do to raw web text before using it to train a before using a pipeline before training an AI on raw internet text before training an AI? before:

options.text: Post it directly to social media

exercises.explain: Just like washing vegetables before cooking them, we must clean raw data before letting our AI learn from it.

cards.title: Don't Eat Raw Data!
cards.body: Imagine trying to learn to speak by reading internet comment sections and spam emails. You would end up talking in gibberish and rudeness! Before an AI learns from the web, we must clean the data first.

cards.title: What is a Data Cleaning Pipeline?
cards.body: A data cleaning pipeline is a step-by-step assembly line that prepares raw text for AI training. Specialized programs called classifiers look at the text and flag spam, hateful language, and nonsense. If a page fails these tests, it is thrown out before the AI ever sees it.

cards.title: The Water Filter
cards.body: Think of raw internet text like water drawn from a muddy river. You wouldn't drink it straight! First, you pass it through filters (classifiers) to catch sand, leaves, and trash. The clean, safe water that comes out the other end is what we feed our AI models.

cards.title: Filtering Spam in Action
cards.body: Imagine downloading a page that says "BUY CHEAP SNEAKERS NOW!!!" ten thousand times. A quality classifier spots this repetitive spam pattern and blocks it. Another page with normal articles and helpful facts gets a green light to pass through.

cards.title: Myth: Humans Read It All
cards.body: You might think AI creators manually read and approve every web page used for training. In reality, the internet is too massive! Instead, they write automated pipelines and use smart helper models to filter billions of pages in seconds.

cards.title: Before vs. After Cleaning
cards.body: See how the pipeline transforms messy raw internet data into clean, safe educational material.

right.text: High-quality books, articles, and clear discussions with junk removed.

left.text: Spam emails, toxic forum posts, random keyboard keyboard smash, and broken links.

exercises.prompt: Why can't we train a helpful AI on raw, unfiltered web text?

options.text: It is already perfectly clean and ready to use.
It contains massive amounts of spam, gibberish, and toxic language.
It only consists of highly accurate encyclopedia articles.
It is written entirely in secret code.

exercises.explain: Raw internet text is full of ads, duplicate spam, hateful comments, and machine-generated nonsense. We must filter it to keep the AI's education clean.

exercises.prompt: True or False: We should let the AI read toxic web pages because it will automatically know to ignore them.
exercises.explain: Gibberish, toxic speech, and repetitive spam must be filtered out during the data cleaning stage, long before the AI starts its core training.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank with the tool used to filter out bad data.
exercises.sentence: To remove spam and gibberish, raw text is sent through automated ________.
exercises.explain: Classifiers are automated tools that read incoming web pages and decide if they contain high-quality text or if they are just junk.

exercises.prompt: One of these bots was trained on raw, unfiltered internet spam. Can you spot it?

options.text: AI Bot A: "To bake a cake, you need flour, sugar, eggs, and butter. Mix them together and bake at 350 degrees."
AI Bot B: "CHEAP FLOUR NOW! CLICK HERE FOR EGGS! BUY SUGAR! BEST DEALS ON BUTTER ONLINE!"
AI Bot C: "Baking is the process of cooking food using dry heat, typically in an oven."

exercises.explain: AI Bot B displays the classic signs of training on raw, unfiltered web data—repeating spammy advertising phrases instead of answering the user's question.

exercises.prompt: How does a data cleaning pipeline prevent an AI from learning toxic habits or gibberish from the raw internet?

options.text: By translating every website into English before reading it
By running text through classifiers that identify and filter out spam and toxic language
By asking human editors to read every single page on the internet
By only using websites that have no punctuation or numbers

exercises.explain: Classifiers act as smart gates. By analyzing the patterns of words, they can instantly identify and throw away toxic content, repetitive spam, and useless gibberish, leaving only high-quality data for the AI to read.
